24: Types of Long-Term Memory: Long-term memory allows systems to retain
25: information across different conversations, providing a deeper level of context and
26: personalization. It can be broken down into three types analogous to human memory:
28: Semantic Memory: Remembering Facts: This involves retaining specific facts
29: and concepts, such as user preferences or domain knowledge. It is used to
30: ground an agent's responses, leading to more personalized and relevant
31: interactions. This information can be managed as a continuously updated user
32: "profile" (a JSON document) or as a "collection" of individual factual
35: Episodic Memory: Remembering Experiences: This involves recalling past
36: events or actions. For AI agents, episodic memory is often used to remember
37: how to accomplish a task. In practice, it's frequently implemented through
